seo.og_title: Logto — Pricing, Developer Experience & Best Use Cases
seo.og_description: Logto for Next.js App Router: Medium learning curve, Free – $599/mo, 20 minutes setup. Open-source core, self-hostable, clean admin console  –  good Auth0 alternative for compliance

hero.headline: Logto: Complete Review for Next.js Developers
hero.subheadline: A thorough breakdown of Logto's pricing, developer experience, and fit for Next.js App Router projects.

intro_paragraphs: Let's talk about Logto. It's priced at Free – $599/mo with a Yes, up to 5,000 MAU free tier and scores 7/10 on Next.js integration. The medium learning curve is a signal that this tool prioritizes depth over speed. I'll help you decide.
who_should_use_paragraphs: I'd recommend Logto to teams that need open-source core with self-hosting option and built-in management api and admin console without the overhead of a full enterprise platform. The 7/10 Next.js score is a reliable signal.
pricing_context: Logto's pricing looks reasonable at first but has a large jump: the free tier covers 5,000 MAU, but the Team plan at $599/mo only covers 30,000 MAU. For a growing SaaS that crosses 30K users, you are looking at the Enterprise tier (custom pricing, likely $1,000+/mo). Self-hosting eliminates the per-user cost but introduces infrastructure costs ($30-100/mo for a small server) plus ongoing maintenance. Clerk at $199/mo for 20K users offers better value and better Next.js DX. Choose Logto if you need self-hosting for compliance reasons; choose Clerk for cost-effective managed auth.

faq.question: How much does Logto cost?
faq.answer: Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. Logto's pricing looks reasonable at first but has a large jump: the free tier covers 5,000 MAU, but the Team plan at $599/mo only covers 30,000 MAU. Factor in your expected MAU when evaluating total cost.

faq.question: How long does it take to set up Logto with Next.js?
faq.answer: Setup time is approximately 20 minutes with a medium learning curve. js SDK that handles the standard authentication flow: redirect users to Logto's hosted sign-in page, handle the callback, and manage sessions.
